translated: And when the man saw that he could not prevail against him, he touched the hollow of his thigh, and the hollow of Jacob's thigh was put out of joint as he wrestled with him.

ref: The king called the first official back in and said, “You're an evil man! When you begged for mercy, I said you did not have to pay back a cent.
text: Amo̱se̱ ne̱ owure‑o a te̱e̱ ane̱ŋ a ke̱yaafɔre̱ amo̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Kuŋurole̱ŋ wuye ke̱yaafɔre̱ e̱ gye̱ fo̱. Ko̱kɔ ne̱ fo̱ de mo̱‑ɔ kyɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ bɛɛɛ? Amaa fo̱ a ko̱re̱ mo̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ nyite fo̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ a taa fo̱ ko̱kɔ amo̱ bo̱ ke fo̱.
translated: Moses and his father-in-law were talking and his father-in-law said to him. And he said to him saying, 'Why are you the only one judging them? But why do you sit alone all day? But if you listen to me what I will tell you, I will help you so that it will be good for you.

ref: I say if your wife has not committed some terrible sexual sin, you must not divorce her to marry someone else. If you do, you are unfaithful.”
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ɔnyare̱ ma kine mò̱ ka ke̱tɔ ke̱maa se̱, amɔ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ a kyɔ le̱e̱ mò̱ kamɛɛ fe̱raa. Ne̱ n gye̱ ane̱ŋ, ɔnyare̱‑ɔ ya waare̱e̱ ɔkye̱e̱ baŋbaŋ, ɔ waa bɔye̱. Mò̱ ke̱be̱e̱ waare̱e̱ ɔkye̱e̱ baŋbaŋ‑ɔ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ a waa kakye̱e̱kpa‑ɔ.”
translated: Moses, the law that you gave us says that if a man's brother dies and leaves a wife but no children, the man should marry the widow. Now then, there were seven brothers. The first one married and died without having children. The second one married the widow, and he also died. The same thing happened to the seven brothers—they all died without leaving any children.

ref: “Which ones?” the man asked. Jesus answered, “Do not murder. Be faithful in marriage. Do not steal. Do not tell lies about others.
text: Ne̱ ɔnyare̱‑ɔ a bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ mbraa?” Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mbraa ne̱ ŋ yɛ, ‘Ɔko̱ ma mɔɔ mò̱ bɛɛko̱, ɔko̱ ma waa kakye̱e̱kpa, ɔko̱ ma kaŋ ywii, ɔko̱ ma kaŋ te̱ŋ mò̱ bɛɛko̱ kanɔ,
translated: And the young man asked him saying, "Which ones?" And Jesus answered him saying, "The ones that say, 'You shall not kill, you shall not commit adultery, you shall not steal, you shall not bear false witness,

ref: But many who are now first will be last, and many who are last will be first.
text: Amaa ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ i kii nsii-amɛɛ, na bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ nsii-amɛɛ mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ mɔ kii agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱.”
translated: But they did not want to give the elders food, and they did not want to give food to the elders.

ref: Jesus replied, “If you want to be perfect, go sell everything you own! Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come and be my follower.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ fo̱ kii se̱sɛ timaa lo̱we̱, amo̱ fe̱raa, nare̱ ya fe ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ fo̱ de‑o, na fo̱ taa atanne̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ bo̱ sa atiripo̱. Fo̱ ya waa ane̱ŋ fe̱raa, Wuribware̱ e̱ kpa kapo̱tɛɛ a ɔ bo̱ ye̱ra so̱so̱ sa fo̱. Na fo̱ bo̱ gya mo̱ se̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ.”
translated: Then Jesus said to them, "You should not think that I have come to destroy the law, but rather, I have come to fulfill it completely, and you will keep it all until heaven and earth pass away. You should know rather, that God will give great reward to those who do it. And you will enter into my kingdom."

ref: After he had agreed to pay them the usual amount for a day's work, he sent them off to his vineyard.
text: Ne̱ ɔ yɛ ɔ sa bamo̱ siidii kudu na bo̱ yɔ ya suŋ sa mò̱ kake amo̱ pɛɛɛ. Ne̱ baa sure si, ne̱ baa yɔ mò̱ ko̱dɔɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ ya suŋ.
translated: If you are a true believer, you should not let anything prevent you from praying. If they refuse, if they do not allow you to pray.

ref: They said, “The ones who were hired last worked for only one hour. But you paid them the same that you did us. And we worked in the hot sun all day long!”
text: Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, ‘Anyare̱ mɔ ne̱ baa ba fo̱ ko̱dɔɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ mfe̱e̱ saŋ ne̱ kyɔwe̱ e̱ kpa a ɔ lo̱we̱‑ɔ a suŋ kusuŋ dɔŋhwe̱re̱e̱ ko̱ŋko̱ ne̱e̱. Ne̱ ane̱ mɔ ane̱ a kpo̱ne̱ suŋ kusuŋ ibiri mɔ pɛɛɛ na kyɔwe̱ a da ane̱. Amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ gbaa fo̱ a ka ane̱ aa bamo̱ atanne̱ ko̱ŋko̱ dɛɛ ne̱e̱.’
translated: They said, 'God has given us this land as our inheritance and the animals that live in it are for our benefit. And we have the right to use all the animals that God has given us. But we must not kill them carelessly at all.'

ref: Don't I have the right to do what I want with my own money? Why should you be jealous, if I want to be generous?”
text: Mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ atanne̱ ne̱e̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ n de kpa fe̱yɛ m ba amo̱ bo̱ waa ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ. Bɛɛɛ a bo̱ fo̱ gya fe̱yɛ mo̱ a waa ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱?’ ”
translated: You are completely foolish. How can you say that whoever touches you will die when you have not died yourself? Or are you saying that you are an immortal?

ref: So they grabbed him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ baa kra mò̱ kayaagyi amo̱, ne̱ baa gyiiri mò̱ le̱e̱ ko̱dɔɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ baa mɔɔ mò̱.
translated: Moses went and returned to his father-in-law, and told him all that had happened, and he blessed him.

ref: Jesus replied, “You surely know that the Scriptures say, ‘The stone the builders tossed aside is now the most important stone of all. This is something the Lord has done, and it is amazing to us.’
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Kase̱ŋtiŋ ne̱e̱. Mo̱ a kɔɔre̱ gyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ a kare̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ke̱fo̱re̱ serisɛ ko̱ ne̱ apwɛɛpo̱ de bo̱ pwɛɛ ne̱ baa kine ke̱mo̱ bo̱ twe̱e̱‑ɔ, ya ko̱ŋ bo̱ kii ke̱mo̱ ne̱ kaa le̱ŋ lɔŋ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ bo̱ ye̱ra‑ɔ. Wuribware̱ ya sa ne̱ aa ba ane̱ŋ, ne̱ aa kpe̱ŋ ane̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ke̱baŋse̱.’ ”
translated: Then Jesus said, "Truly I tell you. If you go and tell your brother about his sin against you, 'If he listens to you and repents and you forgive him, then you have gained your brother and he will be with you in peace and you will be together. But if he does not come and does not accept it, then take witnesses.'"

ref: Jesus said to the crowds and to his disciples:

translated: And Jesus went and preached to the people in the synagogues and cast out their demons.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You lock people out of the kingdom of heaven. You won't go in yourselves, and you keep others from going in.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱ na Farisii awuye‑o mɔ, ŋ yɛ ɛɛɛ, mo̱ne̱ i ŋu ase̱ŋ ŋke ŋko̱ fɛɛ. Nnɔ ŋnyɔ ŋnyɔ awuye e̱ gye̱ mo̱ne̱. Mfaanɛɛ se̱‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ i gyiiri mo̱ne̱ ke̱se̱bɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ.]
translated: Some of the Pharisees and teachers of the law who were there said to him, "Teacher, we want to see a sign from you." But he answered them saying, "An evil and adulterous generation asks for a sign, but no sign will be given to it except the sign of the prophet Jonah, and just as Jonah was three days and three nights in the belly of the great fish, so will the Son of Man be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth."

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You're like tombs that have been whitewashed. On the outside they are beautiful, but inside they are full of bones and filth.
text: Mo̱ne̱ Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱ na Farisii awuye mɔ, m be̱e̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ gye̱ nnɔ ŋnyɔ ŋnyɔ awuye ne̱e̱. Mo̱ne̱ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱gye̱raŋta‑ɔ. Ke̱mo̱ so̱so̱ ne̱ ba ba siimeetii bo̱ laa, ne̱ kaa waa daŋ. Amaa swe̱e̱re̱‑ɔ kaase̱ kifuniŋ da ki i gyo.
translated: When the Pharisees and teachers of the law saw this, they said to him that he eats with tax collectors and sinners. When he heard this, he answered them. Healthy people do not need a doctor, but sick people do. However, go and learn what this means.

ref: and when I was naked, you gave me clothes to wear. When I was sick, you took care of me, and when I was in jail, you visited me.”
text: Saŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ m buŋ aprakɛɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a sa mo̱ atɔ, ne̱ mo̱ a buŋ. Saŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a ŋu mo̱ na mo̱ e̱ lɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a kya mo̱‑rɔ. Saŋ ne̱ baa tii mo̱ tiikpa‑ɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a bo̱ laa mo̱.’
translated: If you do not give him your friend, and you refuse to give him, and you do not give him. If you do not give him what he wants, and you keep it from him. If someone comes to you at midnight and you refuse to give it to him.

ref: The high priest then tore his robe and said, “This man claims to be God! We don't need any more witnesses! You have heard what he said.
text: Amo̱ fe̱raa ne̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ‑ɔ a ba kaduŋfwii bo̱ kyaŋ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ waagya‑rɔ. Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Ɔnyare̱ mɔ a tɔwe̱ abuse̱ŋse̱ŋ. Ane̱ maa lɛɛ kpa a ɔko̱ tɔwe̱ ko̱tɔko̱ pwɛɛ a ane̱ dɛɛ ŋu fe̱yɛ o gyi ke̱pɔ. Mo̱ne̱ a nu ase̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ bo̱ kye Wuribware̱‑ɔ, ɔ yɛ mò̱ aa Wuribware̱ ta.
translated: They went to the chief's house and found him sitting outside in the morning. And he said, "God has given me three sons. text: Saŋ ne̱ asoogyaa awuye‑o de Yeesuu ba yɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa gyaŋŋaa ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ Kireenee maŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Simɔŋ‑ɔ. Ne̱ asoogyaa awuye‑o a ye̱re̱ mò̱ waa‑rɔ fe̱yɛ ɔ so̱rɔ Yeesuu a kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ.
translated: When they were taking Jesus away, they met a man coming from the countryside, and his name was Simon from Cyrene. And the soldiers forced him to carry Jesus' cross.

ref: They made a crown out of thorn branches and placed it on his head, and they put a stick in his right hand. The soldiers knelt down and pretended to worship him. They made fun of him and shouted, “Hey, you king of the Jews!”
text: Ne̱ baa ba iwu bo̱ lo̱ kuwurepa bo̱ buŋ mò̱. Ne̱ baa taa kayii nare̱se̱sɛ bo̱ waa mò̱ kigyisesare̱e̱‑rɔ. Ne̱ baa le̱e̱ ba gyɔŋŋɔ mò̱ ayaa‑rɔ ba waa mò̱ ŋwaagyise̱ŋŋe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Aŋsuma, nana, Yudaa awuye owure.”

translated: And Joseph took the body of Jesus and wrapped it in clean linen cloth. And he laid it in his own new tomb.

text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ‑ɔ a sa se̱ŋsa gywii akye̱e̱‑ɔ. Ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ ma sa a kufu nya mo̱ne̱. Ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ i buwi mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa Yeesuu ne̱ baa da mò̱ bo̱ me̱ra kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱.
translated: Then the angel spoke to the women who had come. He said, "You should not be afraid. I know that you are looking for Jesus who was nailed on the cross and died."

ref: Now hurry! Tell his disciples he has been raised to life and is on his way to Galilee. Go there, and you will see him. This is what I came to tell you.”
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, mbe̱yɔmɔ, mo̱nꞌ nare̱ me̱naŋ me̱naŋ ya tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a kyiŋŋi mò̱ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro, ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ ɔ gye̱ bamo̱ ŋkpɛɛ o yii Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱. Mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ ba ŋu mò̱. Mo̱ ase̱ŋ a lo̱we̱.”
translated: Therefore, brothers, select from among you seven men of good reputation, full of the Holy Spirit and wisdom, whom we may appoint over this task, while we brothers will devote ourselves to prayer and to the ministry of the word. This proposal pleased the whole group.

text: Mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ kare̱ fe̱yɛ ako̱ŋ a de Deefid mò̱ aa mò̱ anare̱bɛɛpo̱‑ɔ, oo lwee Wuribware̱ suŋkpa, ne̱ baa sa mò̱ bodobodoo akuri ne̱ baa bo̱ lɔŋŋɔ Wuribware̱‑ɔ, hare̱e̱ ɔɔ saŋ e̱ko̱ sa mò̱ anare̱bɛɛpo̱‑ɔ, na a mo̱ŋ gye̱ mbraa kikye aaa? translated: When you were small and insignificant in your own eyes, did not the Lord anoint you king over Israel, and the Lord delivered you from the hand of Saul, and gave you your master's house and your master's wives into your keeping, and gave you the house of Israel and Judah. text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔbɔnsam, nare̱ le̱e̱‑rɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ baa be̱e̱ kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱nꞌ suŋ mo̱ne̱ nyaŋpe̱ Wuribware̱ wo̱re̱ kpeŋ.’ ”
translated: "Jesus answered him, "It is written, 'Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that comes from the mouth of God.'"

ref: Right then the two brothers dropped their nets and went with him.

ref: Jesus walked on until he saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat with their father, mending their nets. Jesus asked them to come with him.

ref: God blesses those people who grieve. They will find comfort!

translated: When people do evil things in the world, they are not fearing God who created all people.

translated: Jesus said, "Truly I tell you, if you have faith and do not doubt, not only will you do what was done to the fig tree, but even if you say to this mountain, 'Be lifted up and thrown into the sea,' it will happen. And whatever you ask in prayer, if you believe, you will receive it."

ref: You know our ancestors were told, “Don't use the Lord's name to make a promise unless you are going to keep it.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kya mò̱ atɔ ke̱kaapo̱‑ɔ se̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ a be̱e̱ nu fe̱yɛ de̱daa‑ɔ baa tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ ma waa e̱taŋ na ɔ laŋŋe̱ mò̱ kanɔ. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔko̱ e̱ waa e̱taŋ fe̱yɛ ɔ waa‑ɔ, ɔ waa.
translated: And Jesus said to his disciples, "Truly I say to you, it is hard for a rich person to enter the kingdom of heaven. Again I say to you, it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter the kingdom of God."

ref: You have heard people say, “Love your neighbors and hate your enemies.”
text: Mo̱ne̱ a be̱e̱ nu fe̱yɛ baa tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ɔke̱maa kpa mò̱ bɛɛko̱ ase̱ŋ, na o kisi mò̱ do̱ŋ.’
translated: They will say to the mountains, 'Fall on us,' and to the hills, 'Cover us.'

ref: Then you will be acting like your Father in heaven. He makes the sun rise on both good and bad people. And he sends rain for the ones who do right and for the ones who do wrong.
text: na bo̱ ŋu fe̱yɛ kase̱ŋtiŋ si mo̱ne̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱, mo̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ, mò̱ gyi‑ana. Bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ sa ne̱ mò̱ kyɔwe̱ e̱ we̱e̱ ase̱sɛ bɔye̱ na atimaa si kyɛɛkyɛɛ. Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ bware̱ e̱ ba ɔ we̱e̱ bamo̱ ne̱ ba waa itimaa‑o na bamo̱ ne̱ ba waa e̱bɔye̱‑ɔ atɔ se̱.

translated: And the young men who had grown up with him spoke to him, saying, "Thus you shall say to this people who spoke to you, saying, 'Your father made our yoke heavy, but you make it lighter for us.'"

text: Mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ ne̱ ɔdɔɔpo̱‑ɔ ayaafɔre̱ a bo̱ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ane̱ nyaŋpe̱, ne̱e̱ fo̱ a ŋo̱nyaŋ ayaabraagyi timaa ne̱e̱ ɛ? Ne̱ mfe̱ne̱ ne̱ e̱fa bɔye̱ mɔ a le̱e̱?’
translated: The child and the old man asked the people in the town saying, 'What is this, is it not a wonderful thing that has happened? And how did it come to be like this?'

ref: “An enemy did this,” he replied. His servants then asked, “Do you want us to go out and pull up the weeds?”
text: Ne̱ oo lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ɔdo̱ŋ ko̱ ya waa amo̱‑ɔ.’ Ne̱ baa be̱e̱ bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Amɔ fo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ane̱ ya kyuwi e̱fa‑ɔ le̱e̱ yaabraa amo̱‑rɔ aaa?’
translated: And they said to him, 'Grant us to sit, one at your right hand and one at your left, in your glory.' But Jesus said to them, 'You do not know what you are asking. Are you able to drink the cup that I drink, or to be baptized with the baptism with which I am baptized?'

ref: After Jesus left the crowd and went inside, his disciples came to him and said, “Explain to us the story about the weeds in the wheat field.”
text: Ne̱ ŋkee Yeesuu a le̱e̱ lamaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ lwee lɔŋ‑nɔ. Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a ba mò̱ ase̱ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Kaapo̱ ane̱ e̱fa kitee‑o kaase̱.”
translated: When Jesus had finished teaching them, he told his disciples to go and tell the people, "The kingdom of heaven is near."

ref: Then he will throw them into a flaming furnace, where people will cry and grit their teeth in pain.
text: Na mo̱ mbɔɔ‑ɔ lee bamo̱ le̱e̱ mo̱ kuwure‑o‑ro, na bo̱ twe̱e̱ bamo̱ waa ke̱de̱e̱kpɛɛtekii‑ro. Mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ ne̱ ba su, na ba duŋ nnɔ.
translated: When they saw him coming towards them, before he reached them, they conspired against him. The thing that they did, they did it badly.

text: Na ŋkee a saŋ ase̱sɛ timaa‑o, na bamo̱ itimaa‑o sa a bo̱ laŋŋe̱‑rɔ fe̱yɛ kyɔwe̱‑ɔ bamo̱ se̱ Wuribware̱ a kuwure‑o‑ro. Amo̱ e̱ gye̱ saase̱bɛɛ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ɔke̱maa ne̱ o de e̱se̱bɔ‑ɔ, o nu ase̱ŋ mɔ.” Kalɔgyii na atanne̱ kitee
translated: When you reach the town, you will find a young donkey tied there which no one has ever ridden. Untie it and bring it to me. If anyone asks you why you are untying it, tell them that the Lord needs it and will send it back soon." This happened to fulfill what the prophet had said

ref: That's how it will be at the end of time. Angels will come and separate the evil people from the ones who have done right.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ kaye̱ bo̱ gye̱ kɛɛ‑ɔ, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ke̱tɔ e̱ ba. Wuribware̱ a mbɔɔ‑ɔ e̱ ba a bo̱ bo̱ lee ase̱sɛ bɔye̱‑ɔ le̱e̱ atimaa‑o‑ro.
translated: Moses said to them that they should not leave any of it until morning. But some people did not listen to him and left some until morning.

ref: Herod had earlier arrested John and had him chained and put in prison. He did this because John had told him, “It isn't right for you to take Herodias, the wife of your brother Philip.”
text: Mfaanɛɛ ne̱ Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱‑ɔ a wu. Saŋ ko̱, ne̱ Owure Hɛrɔd a kɔɔre̱ mò̱ tire Filipo mò̱ ka Hɛrɔde̱ya waare̱e̱. Ne̱ Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱‑ɔ mɔ a yii owure‑o si iluwi bwe̱e̱tɔ fe̱yɛ, “A mo̱ŋ bware fe̱yɛ fo̱ a kɔɔre̱ fo̱ tire mò̱ ka waare̱e̱.”
translated: The death of John the Baptist. The reason was that King Herod had taken his brother Philip's wife Herodias. And John the Baptist had been telling the king that it was not right, saying, "It is not lawful for you to take your brother's wife."

ref: But when Peter saw how strong the wind was, he was afraid and started sinking. “Save me, Lord!” he shouted.
text: Amaa afwii‑o a da mò̱ nkyu si mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, kufu a nya mò̱, ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ o kirewi. Ne̱ ɔɔ faa‑rɔ tɔwe̱ ɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, mo̱rɔwe̱ mo̱!”
translated: When the woman saw that the tree was good for food and pleasing to the eye, and also desirable for gaining wisdom, she took some of its fruit and ate it. She also gave some to her husband who was with her, and he ate it.

ref: At once, Jesus made his disciples get into a boat and start back across the lake. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away.
text: Amo̱‑ɔ kamɛɛ‑rɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu a sa ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a lwee ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ gye̱ mò̱ ŋkpɛɛ yɔ nkyu‑o nno̱ŋ a bo̱ŋbe̱‑ɔ se̱, ne̱ mò̱‑ɔ mò̱ a sii a ɔ sa lamaŋ‑ɔ kpa a bo̱ yɔ pe̱.
translated: After that, when Jesus had received the sour wine, he said "It is finished," and he bowed his head and gave up his spirit.

ref: They begged him just to let them touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
text: Ne̱ baa ko̱re̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ dabo̱rɔ bamo̱ na bo̱ kpaare̱, bɛɛɛ ɔ sa alɔpo̱‑ɔ kpa na bo̱ dabo̱rɔ mò̱ waagya kanɔ gbaa. Bamo̱ ne̱ baa dabo̱rɔ mò̱ waagya‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a kpaare̱.
translated: If they want to give you something or they are going to do something for you, they will first ask you, but if the person refuses then they will do what they themselves think is good. They will do what they themselves think without asking you.

translated: When Jesus had finished speaking to the people, he said, "The kingdom of heaven is like a man who sowed good seed in his field."

text: “Nte̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ fo̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ maa gya e̱kpa ne̱ ane̱ ade̱daapo̱ a kaapo̱ ane̱‑ɔ se̱? Ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ ane̱ a bise amo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ maa fwe̱e̱ asare̱e̱, pwɛɛ dɛɛ gyi ateese.”
translated: Why do you look at the speck in your brother's eye but do not notice the log in your own eye? Or how can you say to your brother, 'Let me remove the speck from your eye,' when there is a log in your own eye?

ref: Stay away from those Pharisees! They are like blind people leading other blind people, and all of them will fall into a ditch.”
text: Farisii awuye‑o fe̱raa, mo̱ne̱ ma kaŋ be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱. Bo̱ yɛ bo̱ gye̱ te̱napo̱ kayii‑ro akrapo̱ ne̱e̱. Amaa bamo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ mo̱ŋ ke̱e̱. Te̱napo̱ ya kra te̱napo̱ kiyii‑ro a ɔ gye̱ mò̱ ŋkpɛɛ, bamo̱ pɛɛɛ e̱ le̱e̱ a bo̱ le̱e̱ da ke̱maŋtaŋ‑nɔ.”
translated: The Pharisees answered them, "You also have been deceived. Have any of the rulers or Pharisees believed in him? But this crowd that does not know the law is accursed."

ref: Jesus did not say a word. But the woman kept following along and shouting, so his disciples came up and asked him to send her away.
text: Amaa Yeesuu mo̱ŋ be̱ŋŋaa mò̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ oo su ɔ ko̱re̱ mò̱ ane̱ŋ‑aaa. Ne̱ ŋkee Yeesuu agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a bo̱ ko̱re̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ nyaŋpe̱, gya ɔkye̱e̱ amo̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ gya ane̱ se̱ ane̱ŋ‑aaa, na ɔ maa yɔwe̱ e̱lawo̱ mɔ ko̱waa.”
translated: But Jesus did not go with them. He sent them to go and tell his father. And when Jesus' disciples wanted to go with him too, he said, "You stay, go to your homes, and tell them what he has done for you, so that they too may know God's work."

ref: Peter replied, “What did you mean when you talked about the things that make people unclean?”
text: Ne̱ Peetroo yɛ, “Fo̱ yɛ n gye̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ se̱sɛ i gyi ɔ waa kame‑ro‑o e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ sa a ɔ waa iyisi, kaapo̱ ane̱ ke̱mo̱ kaase̱.”
translated: Then Peter said, "You will never wash my feet because you have washed the feet of all these people, but not mine."

text: Mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ ne̱ ase̱sɛ biribiri a ba mò̱ ase̱. Bamo̱ bo̱ko̱ a baa agyabɔɔ na ate̱napo̱ na asare̱e̱ wusɛ awuye na mmu na bamo̱ ne̱ ba lɔ alɔgyi e̱wo̱re̱se̱ e̱wo̱re̱se̱‑ɔ mò̱ akatɔ‑rɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ kya bamo̱.

translated: And he said to them, "Yes, you are right, you are wise, but the thing that will make you wiser is to listen and to do what the elder says."

ref: When Jesus and his disciples were near the town of Caesarea Philippi, he asked them, “What do people say about the Son of Man?”
text: Saŋ ko̱ ne̱ Yeesuu a yɔ ifuri ko̱ ne̱ e̱ maa Kaye̱sare̱ya Filipii maŋ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ. Bamo̱ a naa ba yɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ oo bise mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “E̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ ase̱sɛ e̱ te̱e̱ mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o?”
translated: One day when Jesus and his disciples were in the region of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, saying, "Who do people say that I, the Son of Man, am?"

ref: The disciples answered, “Some people say you are John the Baptist or maybe Elijah or Jeremiah or some other prophet.”
text: Ne̱ baa lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Bo̱ko̱ yɛ fo̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ a akyaamɛɛ ne̱ baa dɛɛ kye̱na‑ɔ ɔko̱ ne̱ oo kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro, fe̱yɛ Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱‑ɔ, bɛɛɛ Iliya, bɛɛɛ Yɛrɛme̱ya, bɛɛɛ bamo̱‑rɔ ɔko̱‑ɔ.”
translated: Some people say about you the Son of God and those who were prophets in the past who have died and come back to life, saying John the Baptist, or Elijah, or Jeremiah, or one of them.

ref: In fact, he has already come. But the people did not recognize him and treated him just as they wanted to. They will soon make the Son of Man suffer in the same way.”
text: Amaa mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ Iliya a kyɔ ba, ne̱ ase̱sɛ mo̱ŋ pini mò̱. Ne̱ baa waa mò̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ba kpa‑ɔ. Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ba waa mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, bo̱rɔkraa.”
translated: But if you say that John is the one who fulfills what was said about Elijah coming, then you will kill me. And they feared him so they did not kill him. So when they did not kill him, he left them and went away, finished.

ref: and said, “Lord, have pity on my son! He has a bad case of epilepsy and often falls into a fire or into water.
text: “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ fo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ ŋu mo̱ gyi nyaŋsɛ mɔ e̱wɛɛ na fo̱ kya mò̱. Ɔ lɔ kigbuŋgbuŋ ne̱e̱, ne̱ ke̱ e̱ tɔɔraa mò̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Saŋ ke̱maa, ke̱ e̱ twe̱e̱ mò̱ ke̱ e̱ da de̱e̱kpa‑rɔ bɛɛɛ nkyu‑ro.
translated: My friend, if you want to do something for me, I want you to help me find work so that I can support myself. He is very strong, and he can do any kind of work. Therefore, if you can help him get a job somewhere in your area.

text: Amaa ma kpa fe̱yɛ twe̱e̱twe̱e̱ akɔɔre̱po̱ mɔ se̱, ase̱sɛ iduŋ fwii ane̱ se̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, yɔ nkyu‑ro, ya twe̱e̱ drɔwa waa‑rɔ. Na fo̱ taa yaa bo̱ ka mo̱ aa fo̱ a Wuribware̱ suŋkpa a twe̱e̱twe̱e̱‑ɔ.”
translated: But I have not spoken these things to you from the beginning, because I was with you. However, now I am going to him who sent me. Yet you do not ask me, 'Where are you going?' But because I have said these things to you, sorrow has filled your hearts and it has made you not want to hear anything about the things I have spoken. But you should know that it is good for you that I go to the Father about these things.

ref: This story will show you what the kingdom of heaven is like: One day a king decided to call in his officials and ask them to give an account of what they owed him.
text: Mo̱nꞌ nu ane̱ŋ ne̱ a du Wuribware̱ se̱ a kuwure‑o‑ro‑o. N gye̱ owure ko̱ ya dɛɛ kye̱na aaa? Ne̱ mò̱ ayaafɔre̱‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ de mò̱ ko̱kɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ waa mò̱ mfɛɛre̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ te̱e̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ a ɔ bo̱ gyaŋŋe̱ na ɔ nya ŋu mò̱ aa bamo̱ mbo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ atanne̱ ne̱ a bo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ na ɔ kɔɔre̱ le̱e̱ bamo̱ ase̱.
translated: You people who have believed in God, why do you say what you do not do? It is very hateful to God when you say things with your mouth that you do not do. And if you want to fight, then stand together in rows like a wall that has been built so that you can help each other in the war and you can defeat your enemies.

ref: The king felt sorry for him and let him go free. He even told the official that he did not have to pay back the money.
text: Ne̱ ŋkee owure‑o a ŋu fe̱yɛ mò̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱ amo̱ ase̱ŋ a waa e̱wɛɛ, ne̱ ɔɔ taa ko̱kɔ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ bo̱ ke mò̱, ne̱ ɔɔ sa mò̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ nare̱.
translated: And when the man saw that he could not prevail against him, he touched the hollow of his thigh, and the hollow of Jacob's thigh was out of joint as he wrestled with him.

text: Amo̱se̱ ne̱ owure‑o a te̱e̱ ane̱ŋ a ke̱yaafɔre̱ amo̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Kuŋurole̱ŋ wuye ke̱yaafɔre̱ e̱ gye̱ fo̱. Ko̱kɔ ne̱ fo̱ de mo̱‑ɔ kyɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ bɛɛɛ? Amaa fo̱ a ko̱re̱ mo̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ nyite fo̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ a taa fo̱ ko̱kɔ amo̱ bo̱ ke fo̱.
translated: Moses and his father-in-law were sitting together and his father-in-law saw him. And he said to him, "Why are you judging the people alone? And you sit from morning until evening? But if you listen to me what I will tell you, I will help you and it will be good for you.

ref: I say if your wife has not committed some terrible sexual sin, you must not divorce her to marry someone else. If you do, you are unfaithful.”
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ɔnyare̱ ma kine mò̱ ka ke̱tɔ ke̱maa se̱, amɔ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ a kyɔ le̱e̱ mò̱ kamɛɛ fe̱raa. translated: Moses, the law that you gave us says that if a man dies and leaves his wife without children, his brother must marry the widow. Now there were seven brothers. ref: “Which ones?” the man asked. Jesus answered, “Do not murder. Be faithful in marriage. Do not steal. Do not tell lies about others.
text: Ne̱ ɔnyare̱‑ɔ a bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ mbraa?” Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mbraa ne̱ ŋ yɛ, ‘Ɔko̱ ma mɔɔ mò̱ bɛɛko̱, ɔko̱ ma waa kakye̱e̱kpa, ɔko̱ ma kaŋ ywii, ɔko̱ ma kaŋ te̱ŋ mò̱ bɛɛko̱ kanɔ,
translated: And the young man asked him saying, "Which ones?" And Jesus answered him saying, "The ones that say, 'You shall not kill, you shall not commit adultery, you shall not steal, you shall not bear false witness,'

ref: But many who are now first will be last, and many who are last will be first.
text: Amaa ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ i kii nsii-amɛɛ, na bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ nsii-amɛɛ mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ mɔ kii agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱.”
translated: But now you have more honor than the priests, and they have more honor than you.

ref: Jesus replied, “If you want to be perfect, go sell everything you own! Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come and be my follower.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ fo̱ kii se̱sɛ timaa lo̱we̱, amo̱ fe̱raa, nare̱ ya fe ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ fo̱ de‑o, na fo̱ taa atanne̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ bo̱ sa atiripo̱. Fo̱ ya waa ane̱ŋ fe̱raa, Wuribware̱ e̱ kpa kapo̱tɛɛ a ɔ bo̱ ye̱ra so̱so̱ sa fo̱. Na fo̱ bo̱ gya mo̱ se̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ.”
translated: Then Jesus said to them, "You should not think that I came to destroy the law, but rather, I came to fulfill everything that is in it, so that you may keep the commandments and do them well. ref: After he had agreed to pay them the usual amount for a day's work, he sent them off to his vineyard.
text: Ne̱ ɔ yɛ ɔ sa bamo̱ siidii kudu na bo̱ yɔ ya suŋ sa mò̱ kake amo̱ pɛɛɛ. Ne̱ baa sure si, ne̱ baa yɔ mò̱ ko̱dɔɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ ya suŋ.
translated: If you are a true believer, you should not be afraid of anything at all. If they beat you, if they kill you, you will go to heaven.

ref: They said, “The ones who were hired last worked for only one hour. But you paid them the same that you did us. And we worked in the hot sun all day long!”
text: Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, ‘Anyare̱ mɔ ne̱ baa ba fo̱ ko̱dɔɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ mfe̱e̱ saŋ ne̱ kyɔwe̱ e̱ kpa a ɔ lo̱we̱‑ɔ a suŋ kusuŋ dɔŋhwe̱re̱e̱ ko̱ŋko̱ ne̱e̱. Ne̱ ane̱ mɔ ane̱ a kpo̱ne̱ suŋ kusuŋ ibiri mɔ pɛɛɛ na kyɔwe̱ a da ane̱. Amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ gbaa fo̱ a ka ane̱ aa bamo̱ atanne̱ ko̱ŋko̱ dɛɛ ne̱e̱.’
translated: They said, 'God has not given us the power to know the day or time when he will come to establish his kingdom. But you will receive power when the Holy Spirit comes upon you. Then you will be my witnesses everywhere.'

ref: Take your money now and go! What business is it of yours if I want to pay them the same that I paid you?
text: To, taa fo̱ kakɔka a fo̱ nare̱ pe̱. Mo̱ ya ka fo̱ aa ɔnyare̱ mɔ ne̱ oo sii kamɛɛ bo̱ dɔɔ mo̱‑ɔ atanne̱ ko̱ŋko̱ gbaa ooo, a mo̱ŋ gye̱ fo̱ ase̱ŋ.
translated: So, when you are sick and you are not well. We will take you to the hospital so that they will give you medicine to drink so that you will get better quickly, and you will become well again.

ref: Don't I have the right to do what I want with my own money? Why should you be jealous, if I want to be generous?”
text: Mo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ atanne̱ ne̱e̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ n de kpa fe̱yɛ m ba amo̱ bo̱ waa ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ. Bɛɛɛ a bo̱ fo̱ gya fe̱yɛ mo̱ a waa ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱?’ ”

translated: Then Jesus answered them, saying, "The hour has come for the Son of Man to be glorified."

text: Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ bo̱re̱‑rɔ i duŋwi mo̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ, hare̱e̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa a ŋ wu. To, mo̱nꞌ kyiŋŋi kye̱na mfe̱e̱.”
translated: And he said to his disciples, "Collect the leftover pieces so that nothing is wasted. So, they filled twelve baskets."

ref: Jesus went back and prayed the same prayer.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ kiŋŋi ya ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ ko̱re̱ ke̱gye̱ŋkpɛɛ‑ɔ.
translated: And he will give you a white stone and he will write a new name on it.

ref: Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane, and he told them, “Sit here while I pray.”
text: Amo̱‑ɔ kamɛɛ‑rɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a ba bane̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Gɛte̱sɛmane̱e̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ kye̱na mfe̱e̱ na ŋ ya ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱.”
translated: After they had sung a hymn, Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane, and he told his disciples, "Sit here while I go over there to pray."

ref: Jesus was still speaking, when Judas the betrayer came up. He was one of the twelve disciples, and a mob of men armed with swords and clubs were with him. They had been sent by the chief priests, the nation's leaders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses.
text: Yeesuu saŋ ɔ sa se̱ŋsa, ne̱ Yudas ne̱ ɔ gye̱ Yeesuu agyase̱po̱ kudu anyɔ‑ɔ ɔko̱‑ɔ a fo̱. Mò̱ aa ase̱sɛ kukwii ko̱ na bamo̱ apaŋ na akyokuŋ ya ba. Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ na Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ na maŋ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ ya suŋ bamo̱.
translated: Jesus knew his time had come, and Judas and his followers who would arrest Jesus were coming. He had told his disciples that they would all scatter and flee. The chief priests and the elders and their followers came together.

ref: All of Jesus' disciples ran off and left him.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a agyase̱po̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a se̱re̱ yɔwe̱ mò̱.
translated: They took Jesus and crucified him there.

ref: Peter replied, “That isn't true! I don't know what you're talking about. I don't have any idea what you mean.” He went out to the gate, and a rooster crowed.
text: Ne̱ Peetroo a swe̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ŋ nyi ase̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ kaase̱.” Ne̱ ɔɔ nare̱ ya ye̱re̱ lɔŋ‑ɔ kibunogyi‑ro. Puri amo̱‑rɔ ne̱ kyaŋare̱ a fo̱re̱.
translated: Then Peter answered and said, "I want to go with you even to death." And he drew his sword from its sheath. He wanted to fight with them.

ref: But even then they did not agree on what they said.
text: Amaa bamo̱ gbaa bamo̱ aye̱ba‑ɔ mo̱ŋ taare̱ ye̱re̱ abɛɛ se̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu i gyi ke̱pɔ‑ɔ.
translated: But when they saw the courage of Peter and John, and perceived that they were uneducated, common men, they were astonished. And they recognized that they had been with Jesus.

ref: The high priest stood up in the council and asked Jesus, “Why don't you say something in your own defense? Don't you hear the charges they are making against you?”
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ‑ɔ a ko̱so̱ ye̱re̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ akatɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ oo bise Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Amɔ fo̱ mo̱ŋ de ase̱ŋ mɔ pɛɛɛ ne̱ ba tɔwe̱ ba kye fo̱‑ɔ kanɔlee aaa?”
translated: And the Pharisees came to test him in his heart, and they asked Jesus, saying, "Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife for any reason?"

ref: Some of the people started spitting on Jesus. They blindfolded him, hit him with their fists, and said, “Tell us who hit you!” Then the guards took charge of Jesus and beat him. Peter Says He Doesn't Know Jesus (Matthew
text: Mfe̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ bo̱ko̱ a le̱e̱ ba twe̱e̱ akyɔne̱ ba we̱e̱ Yeesuu si. Ne̱ baa ba kaprakɛɛ bo̱ buŋ mò̱ akatɔ se̱, na ba da mò̱ akuŋtiŋ. Ŋkee bamo̱ ya da mò̱, na bo̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nsɛ ya da fo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱?” Ne̱ ade̱e̱re̱se̱po̱‑ɔ a taa mò̱ yaa daye̱ mò̱ e̱se̱bɔ‑rɔ abaa. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Peetroo a swe̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ mo̱ŋ nyi Yeesuu‑o (Matiyo
translated: When they came to the crowd, a man came to Jesus. And when he knelt down before him in the market, he greeted him. After he greeted him, the people asked him, "What has happened to you?" And the teacher answered him and told him his problem. Then Peter said that he did not know Jesus.

ref: At once the rooster crowed a second time. Then Peter remembered that Jesus had told him, “Before a rooster crows twice, you will say three times that you don't know me.” So Peter started crying.
text: Mò̱ ke̱de̱ŋ tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ kyaŋare̱ a fo̱re̱ nyɔse̱po̱. Ne̱ oo nyiŋŋi ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱‑ɔ se̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ e̱ swe̱e̱ iluwi e̱sa fe̱yɛ fo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi mo̱ pwɛɛ na kyaŋare̱ dɛɛ fo̱re̱ iluwi e̱nyɔ.” Ne̱ ɔɔ ya su bwe̱e̱tɔ.
translated: They brought the donkey to him, and they put their garments on it. And when he sat on the donkey that they brought to him, he said, "You will see today that you are doing a great thing for me today." And he went on the road.

ref: But the chief priests told the crowd to ask Pilate to free Barabbas.
text: Amo̱ fe̱raa ne̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ a da lamaŋ‑ɔ kanɔ se̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ tɔwe̱ ɛ o lee Bar‑Abas. Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Lee Bar‑Abas.”
translated: They had a notorious prisoner whose name was called Bar-Abas. When they gathered, Pilate asked them saying, "Who do you want me to release for you, Jesus who is called Christ, or Bar-Abas?" And they said, "Bar-Abas."

ref: But Jesus did not answer, and Pilate was amazed. The Death Sentence (Matthew
text: Amaa kanɔ Yeesuu be̱e̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ buŋŋi. Ne̱ gominaa‑o e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ mò̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Romanyi gominaa‑o a sure si fe̱yɛ bo̱ mɔɔ Yeesuu‑o (Matiyo
translated: But when Jesus entered his town. The governor did not like him. And the Roman governor wanted to kill Jesus (Matthew

text: Amo̱ fe̱raa ne̱ baa fɛɛ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, “Da mò̱ bo̱ me̱ra kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ se̱!”
translated: "They said to their father, "Give us our share of the inheritance!"
